Goal: Confrim for every instruction with the .w* suffix (and widening reduction operations), that if vs1 and vs2 are the same register, the encoding is reserved
Feature Description:
Run a widening instruction with sew = {supported}, lmul = {1/2/4}, vs2 = vs1 = v8

Crossing all lmul (except 8 as it implies an emul of 16) confrims that the overlap is recognized for all sizes of register groups and running with v8 keeps register group alignment and removes and possible conflicts with v0
Spec: An encoding that would result in the same vector register being read with two or more different EEWs, including when the vector register appears at different positions within two or more vector register groups, is reserved.

Goal: Confrim for a single instruction with the .w* suffix (and widening reduction operations), every combination of vs1 and vs2 across lmul = {1/2/4/8} is reserved
Feature Description:
With a widdening instruction cross vs1 = {0-31} with vs2 = {0-31} and lmul = {1/2/4} with sew = {supported}

Crossing all lmul (except 8 as it implies an emul of 16) confrims that the overlap is recognized for all sizes of register groups. Although much trapping will occur becuase of incorrect register alignment, this simplifies both the writing of the coverpoint and testing
Spec: An encoding that would result in the same vector register being read with two or more different EEWs, including when the vector register appears at different positions within two or more vector register groups, is reserved.
Normative Rules: EEW; EEW_SEW_LMUL_dep; EMUL; EMUL_SEW_LMUL_dep; EMUL_rsv; vreg_overlap_rsv; vreg_source_EEW_rsv; vwop_vd_eew_emul

Sr No (link to coverage): All vzext and vsext instructions ending in f2

Goal: Confirm that with zero and sign extending instructions ending in "f2," when the vs2 overlaps the bottom half of the vd register group the operation is illegal
Feature Description:
Conduct a widening operation with lmul=2, vd group aligned and vs2 overlapping the bottom half of the vd register group (if lmul is 2 and vd = v0 then vs2 would be v0 for vzext.vf2).

If vd and vs2 registers must overlap in this manner it is illegal
Spec:
A destination vector register group can overlap a source vector register group only if one of the following holds:

The destination EEW is greater than the source EEW, the source EMUL is at least 1, and the overlap is in the highest-numbered part of the destination register group (e.g., when LMUL=8, vzext.vf4 v0, v6 is legal, but a source of v0, v2, or v4 is not).
Normative Rules: EEW; EMUL; EMUL_SEW_LMUL_dep; vreg_lmulge2_op; vreg_overlap_legal; vreg_overlap_rsv; vsext_op; vsext_vd_eew_emul; vsext_vs_eew_emul; vzext_op; vzext_vd_eew_emul; vzext_vs_eew_emul

Sr No (link to coverage): All vzext and vsext instructions ending in f4

If the vd and vs2 registers overlap in this manner it is illegal

A destination vector register group can overlap a source vector register group only if one of the following holds:

Goal: Confirm that with widening instructions (must end in .w*) vs1 = vd is illegal
Feature Description:
Conduct a widening operation with lmul=1, vs1 = vd and vd group aligned. vs2 should not overlap vd

Goal: Confrim that with all widening instructions (ending in .v* and .w* exluding the widening reduction instructions) that EMUL>=16 traps
Feature Description:
Set sew = {supported} and lmul = {8} and confrim that the instruction traps

sew needs to be supported to ensure the instruction doesnt trap due to vill, lmul of 8 will imply an emul of 16 with widening instructions
Spec: The largest vector register group used by an instruction can not be greater than 8 vector registers (i.e., EMUL≤8), and if a vector instruction would require greater than 8 vector registers in a group, the instruction encoding is reserved. For example, a widening operation that produces a widened vector register group result when LMUL=8 is reserved as this would imply a result EMUL=16.

Goal: Confrim that with all narrowing instructions that EMUL>=16 traps
Feature Description:
Set sew = {supported} and lmul = {8} and confrim that the instruction traps

sew needs to be supported to ensure the instruction doesnt trap due to vill, lmul of 8 will imply an emul of 16 with narrowing instructions
Spec: The largest vector register group used by an instruction can not be greater than 8 vector registers (i.e., EMUL≤8), and if a vector instruction would require greater than 8 vector registers in a group, the instruction encoding is reserved. For example, a widening operation that produces a widened vector register group result when LMUL=8 is reserved as this would imply a result EMUL=16.

Goal: Confirm for all widening instructions (.v*, .w* and reduction) that if SEW=ELEN the instruction traps
Feature Description:
Set sew = {8,16,32,64} and lmul = {1}, vd = 8, vs2 = 10, vs1 = 12

Attempt to set all sew as this is simpler than figuring out SEW_MAX, the instruction should only trap for the highest SEW. Registers were chosen to ensure the instruction doesnt trap due to register overlap

Goal: Confirm for all narrowing instructions that if SEW=ELEN the instruction traps
Feature Description:
Set sew = {8,16,32,64} and lmul = {1}, vd = 8, vs2 = 10, vs1 = 12

Attempt to set all sew as this is simpler than figuring out SEW_MAX, the instruction should only trap for the highest SEW. Registers were chosen to ensure the instruction doesnt trap due to register overlap

Goal: Confrim for vrgather instructions that if vs1 and vd overlap that the encoding is reserved
Feature Description:
Set vs1 = vd = {v0-28} and lmul = 4 with vs2 not overlapping

vs2 not overlapping ensures that the encoding is reserved due to vs1 not vs2,  lmul of 4 ensures vrgatherei16 wont be reserved due to emul
Spec: For any vrgather instruction, the destination vector register group cannot overlap with the source vector register groups, otherwise the instruction encoding is reserved.

Goal: Confrim for vrgather.v* instructions that if vs2 and vd overlap that the encoding is reserved
Feature Description:
Set vs2 = vd = {v0-28} and lmul = 4 with vs1 not overlapping

vs1 not overlapping ensures that the encoding is reserved due to vs2 not vs1, lmul of 4 ensures vrgatherei16 wont be reserved due to emul
Spec: For any vrgather instruction, the destination vector register group cannot overlap with the source vector register groups, otherwise the instruction encoding is reserved.

Goal: Confirm if vrgatherei16 implies an emul of 16 the encoding is reserved
Feature Description:
run lmul = 8 and sew = 8 and vd != vs1 and vd != vs2 (all group aligned)

lmul = 8 and sew = 8 are the only values that can produce an emul of 16. Register restrictions ensure that encoding isnt reserved for other reasons
Spec: The vrgather.vv form uses SEW/LMUL for both the data and indices. The vrgatherei16.vv form uses SEW/LMUL for the data in vs2 but EEW=16 and EMUL = (16/SEW)*LMUL for the indices in vs1

Goal: Confirm if vrgatherei16 implies an emul of 1/16 the encoding is reserved
Feature Description:
run {lmul, sew} = {(1/8, 32), (1/4, 64)} and vd != vs1 and vd != vs2 (all group aligned)

These are the two situations tha produce an emul = 1/16. Register restrictions ensure that encoding isnt reserved for other reasons
Spec: The vrgather.vv form uses SEW/LMUL for both the data and indices. The vrgatherei16.vv form uses SEW/LMUL for the data in vs2 but EEW=16 and EMUL = (16/SEW)*LMUL for the indices in vs1

Goal: Confrim for a "masked vector instruction" that vd = v0 is reserved
Feature Description:
Run a masked vector instruction (mask enabled) with lmul = 1 and vd = v0, vd != vs1, vd != vs2, vs2 != vs1

run with lmul of 1 to ensure all other vector registers are on group and the instruction is not reserved for that reason (same with registers equaling each other)
Spec: The destination vector register group for a masked vector instruction cannot overlap the source mask register (v0), unless the destination vector register is being written with a mask value (e.g., compares) or the scalar result of a reduction. These instruction encodings are reserved.

Goal: Confirm any NFIELDS * EEW violation is checked, run on all instructions with nf and eew
Feature Description: Run segment load/store instructions with NFIELDS and EEW combinations where NFIELDS * EEW exceeds the implementation limit, across all segment load/store instruction variants
Spec: The EMUL setting must be such that EMUL * NFIELDS ≤ 8, otherwise the instruction encoding is reserved.

Goal: Confirm for a masked vector instruction that vd = v0 with LMUL > 1 is reserved
Feature Description:
Run a masked vector instruction (mask enabled) with lmul = {2, 4, 8} and vd = v0, vd != vs1, vd != vs2, vs2 != vs1

Complements cp_ssstrictv_masking_vd_eq_v0 (LMUL=1) by testing higher LMUL values where the vd register group still overlaps v0
Normative Rules: LMUL; V_masked; vreg_overlap_rsv; vreg_vmask; vreg_vmask_rsv; vtype-lmul_val

Goal: Confirm segment load/store traps when register numbers increment past 31
Feature Description: Run a segment load/store with LMUL=1 and NFIELDS/vd combinations where vd + NFIELDS > 32 (e.g., NFIELDS=2 vd=31, NFIELDS=4 vd=29, NFIELDS=8 vd=25)

Goal: Confirm indexed segment load traps when destination groups overlap vs2 index register
Feature Description: Run an indexed segment load with LMUL=1 and vd=8, with vs2 set to each destination register in the segment range (vs2=vd through vs2=vd+NFIELDS-1) across NFIELDS=2..8

Goal: Confirm whole register load/store traps for non-power-of-2 nf and unaligned vd
Feature Description: Run a whole register load/store with nf encoding non-power-of-2 register counts (NREG=3,5,6,7). Also run with valid NREG (2,4,8) but vd not aligned to NREG
Bins: 4 non-pow2 nf bins + 16 vd off-group (NREG=2) + 24 vd off-group (NREG=4) + 28 vd off-group (NREG=8)

Goal: Confirm vector FP instructions trap when frm contains reserved rounding mode
Feature Description: Run a vector floating-point instruction with frm set to each invalid rounding mode (5, 6, 7). Also run with vl=0 and with vstart >= vl to confirm the reservation applies regardless of element activity
Bins: 3 frm bins x 3 conditions (normal / vl=0 / vstart>=vl)

Goal: Confirm vector FP instructions trap when operand EEW is not a supported FP type width
Feature Description: Run a vector FP instruction with SEW=8 (never a valid FP width). Also run with SEW=64 when FLEN=32 (no D extension). Test under normal conditions, with vl=0, and with vstart >= vl
Bins: 1-2 sew bins x 3 conditions (normal / vl=0 / vstart>=vl)

Goal: Confirm vzext instructions trap when source EEW is unsupported or source EMUL is below minimum
Feature Description: Run vzext.vf2/vf4/vf8 with SEW/LMUL combinations where source EEW (SEW/N) < 8 or source EMUL (LMUL/N) < 1/8. For vf2: SEW=8 or LMUL=mf8. For vf4: SEW<=16 or LMUL<=mf4. For vf8: SEW<=32 or LMUL<=mf2
Bins: 3 src_eew crosses (1+2+3 sew bins) + 3 src_emul crosses (1+2+3 lmul bins)

Goal: Confirm vadc/vsbc trap when encoded with vm=1 (unmasked)
Feature Description: Run a vadc or vsbc instruction with vm=1 (the reserved unmasked encoding)
Bins: 2 funct6 bins (vadc and vsbc) x vm=1

Goal: Confirm vadc/vsbc trap when vd is v0
Feature Description: Run a vadc or vsbc instruction with the destination register set to v0
Bins: 2 funct6 bins (vadc and vsbc) x vd=v0

Goal: Confirm vmv.v.v traps when vs2 is not v0
Feature Description: Run a vmv.v.v instruction with vs2 set to any register other than v0

Goal: Confirm widening/narrowing FP instructions trap when wider operand EEW is not a supported FP type
Feature Description: Run a widening or narrowing vector FP instruction with SEW=32 when FLEN=32 (no D extension), so the wider operand EEW=64 is unsupported. Test under normal conditions, with vl=0, and with vstart >= vl
Bins: 1 sew bin x 3 conditions (normal / vl=0 / vstart>=vl)
Normative Rules: V_dependent_F_D; V_fp_EEW_IEEE_nsupported_rsv; V_fp_eew_rsv; Vf_requrires_Vx; Zve32f_dependent_F; vstart_vl_dep; vwop_vd_eew_emul

Goal: Confirm vfmv.v.f traps when vs2 is not v0
Feature Description: Run a vfmv.v.f instruction with vs2 set to any register other than v0

Goal: Confirm mask logical instructions trap when vm=0
Feature Description: Run a vector mask logical instruction (vmand/vmnand/vmandn/vmxor/vmor/vmnor/vmorn/vmxnor) with vm=0 (masked encoding)

Goal: Confirm vid.v traps when vs2 is not v0
Feature Description: Run a vid.v instruction with vs2 set to any register other than v0

Goal: Confirm vmv.x.s and vmv.s.x trap when vm=0
Feature Description: Run a vmv.x.s or vmv.s.x instruction with vm=0 (masked encoding)

Goal: Confirm vfmv.f.s and vfmv.s.f trap when vm=0
Feature Description: Run a vfmv.f.s or vfmv.s.f instruction with vm=0 (masked encoding)

Goal: Confirm vrgather traps when destination register group overlaps vs2 register group
Feature Description: Run a vrgather instruction where the vd register group overlaps the vs2 register group, at each LMUL={1,2,4,8}

Goal: Confirm vrgather.vv traps when destination register group overlaps vs1 register group
Feature Description: Run a vrgather.vv instruction where the vd register group overlaps the vs1 register group, at each LMUL={1,2,4,8}

Goal: Confirm vcompress traps when destination register group overlaps source register group
Feature Description: Run a vcompress instruction where the vd register group overlaps the vs2 register group, at each LMUL={1,2,4,8}

Goal: Confirm vcompress traps when destination register group overlaps source mask register (v0)
Feature Description: Run a vcompress instruction where the vd register group overlaps v0 (the source mask register), at each LMUL={1,2,4,8}

Goal: Confirm whole register move traps when simm[4:0] is a reserved value
Feature Description: Run a vmv<nr>r.v instruction with simm[4:0] set to a value other than 0/1/3/7 (the reserved encodings)
Bins: 28 reserved simm bins (all simm values except 0/1/3/7)

Goal: Confirm whole register move traps when both vd and vs2 are misaligned for the register group size
Feature Description: Run vmv2r.v/vmv4r.v/vmv8r.v with both vd and vs2 unaligned for the NREG group size (not divisible by NREG)
Bins: 3 NREG variants x all unaligned vd x all unaligned vs2

Goal: Confirm vcsr top XLEN-3 bits are reserved and read as zero
Feature Description: Write vcsr via CSRRx with non-zero values in bits [XLEN-1:3], confirm they read back as zero
Bins: 1 bin (upper bits zero after write with non-zero source)

Goal: Confirm masked vector instruction traps when vd=v0 at any LMUL
Feature Description: Run a masked vector instruction with vd=v0 at each LMUL={1,2,4,8} where vd != vs1 and vd != vs2
Bins: 4 LMUL bins (vd=v0 masked at each LMUL)

Goal: Confirm vector instructions trap when register specifier is not aligned to LMUL group size
Feature Description: Run a vector instruction with LMUL={2,4,8} where vd, vs1, or vs2 is not aligned to the register group size (e.g., odd register for LMUL=2)
Bins: 9 crosses: 3 LMUL values x 3 register operands (vd, vs1, vs2), each with all unaligned registers

Goal: Confirm that all load/store trap when violating EEW = 2 * ELEN
Feature Description: Run vector load/store instructions with EEW encoded in the width field set to 2 * ELEN (e.g., if ELEN=64, attempt EEW=128 encoding)
Spec: Vector loads and stores have an EEW encoded directly in the instruction. If the EEW is not a supported width, the instruction encoding is reserved.

Goal: Confirm that all load/store trap when EMUL = 16
Feature Description: Run vector load/store instructions with SEW/LMUL/EEW combinations producing (EEW/SEW)*LMUL = 16 (e.g., EEW=64, SEW=8, LMUL=2)
Spec: If the EMUL would be out of range (EMUL>8 or EMUL<1/8), the instruction encoding is reserved

Goal: Confirm that all load/store trap when EMUL = 1/16
Feature Description: Run vle8/vse8 with {SEW=16/LMUL=1/8, SEW=32/LMUL=1/4, SEW=64/LMUL=1/2} to produce EMUL=1/16 (out of range, must trap)
Spec: If the EMUL would be out of range (EMUL>8 or EMUL<1/8), the instruction encoding is reserved

Goal: Confirm that using 16 registers with strided insturctions is reserved
Feature Description: Run segment load/store instructions with EMUL and NFIELDS combinations where EMUL * NFIELDS > 8 (e.g., EMUL=2, NFIELDS=5)
Spec: The EMUL setting must be such that EMUL * NFIELDS ≤ 8, otherwise the instruction encoding is reserved.

Goal: Confirm vslide1up traps when destination register group overlaps source register group
Feature Description: Run vslide1up.vx with LMUL={1,2,4,8} where vd register group overlaps vs2 register group
Bins: 4 crosses: LMUL=1 (vd==vs2), LMUL=2 (group overlap), LMUL=4 (group overlap), LMUL=8 (group overlap)
Spec: The vslide1up instruction requires that the destination vector register group does not overlap the source vector register group. Otherwise the instruction encoding is reserved.

Goal: Confirm that vcompress with non-zero vstart raises an illegal instruction exception
Feature Description: Run a vcompress instruction with vstart != 0
Bins: 1 cross: vstart nonzero × trap × vcompress encoding
Spec: Executing a vcompress instruction with a non-zero vstart raises an illegal instruction exception.

Goal: Confirm that a trap on vcompress is always reported with vstart of 0
Feature Description: Run a vcompress instruction that traps and verify vstart is 0 after the trap
Bins: 1 cross: vcompress encoding × trap × vstart=0 after
Spec: A trap on a vcompress instruction is always reported with a vstart of 0.

Goal: Confirm that vmv<nr>r.v instructions trap with off-group base registers
Feature Description:
Run vmv2r.v, vmv4r.v, vmv8r.v with vd or vs2 not aligned to NREG.
For vmv2r.v: vd or vs2 must be divisible by 2
For vmv4r.v: vd or vs2 must be divisible by 4
For vmv8r.v: vd or vs2 must be divisible by 8
Spec: Whole register move: The source and destination vector register numbers must be aligned appropriately for the vector register group size, and encodings with other vector register numbers are reserved.

Feature Description: Set LMUL=2 and execute an indexed segment load where vs2 falls within one of the destination register groups (each spanning EMUL=2 registers)
Spec: For vector indexed segment loads, the destination vector register groups cannot overlap the source vector register group (specified by vs2), else the instruction encoding is reserved.

Goal: Confirm CSR addresses 0x00B-0x00E raise illegal instruction exceptions
Feature Description: Attempt to read/write CSR addresses 0x00B, 0x00C, 0x00D, 0x00E (reserved for future vector CSRs) and confirm each raises an illegal instruction trap

Goal: Confirm vsetvli/vsetivli with vsew[2:0] = 1xx sets vill

Goal: Confirm vsetvli with unsupported LMUL/SEW ratio sets vill
Feature Description: Run vsetvli with LMUL and SEW combinations where LMUL < minSEW/ELEN (the ratio is unsupported), and verify vill is set

Goal: Confirm vector load/store instructions trap when EEW < SEWMIN

Spec: Vector loads and stores have an EEW encoded directly in the instruction. If the EEW is not a supported width, the instruction encoding is reserved.

Goal: Confirm vector load/store instructions trap when mew bit is set
Feature Description: Run a vector load or store instruction with mew=1 (bit 28 of instruction) and confirm the encoding is reserved

Goal: Confirm vzext.vf2 traps when source EEW < 8 or source EMUL < 1/8
Feature Description: Run vzext.vf2 with SEW=8 (source EEW=4, unsupported). Also run with LMUL=mf8 (source EMUL=1/16, below minimum)

Goal: Confirm vzext.vf4 traps when source EEW < 8 or source EMUL < 1/8
Feature Description: Run vzext.vf4 with SEW=8 (EEW=2) or SEW=16 (EEW=4). Also run with LMUL=mf8 (EMUL=1/32) or LMUL=mf4 (EMUL=1/16)

Goal: Confirm vzext.vf8 traps when source EEW < 8 or source EMUL < 1/8
Feature Description: Run vzext.vf8 with SEW=8 (EEW=1) or SEW=16 (EEW=2) or SEW=32 (EEW=4). Also run with LMUL=mf8 (EMUL=1/64) or LMUL=mf4 (EMUL=1/32) or LMUL=mf2 (EMUL=1/16)

Feature Description: Run a vcompress instruction with vm=0 (masked encoding)

Feature Description: Run vsext.vf2/vf4/vf8 with SEW values where source EEW = SEW/N < 8, or with fractional LMUL values where source EMUL = LMUL/N < 1/8

Goal: Confirm vzext/vsext instructions trap when source EMUL < 1 and vs2 overlaps vd group
Feature Description: Run vzext/vsext.vf2 with LMUL=1, vf4 with LMUL={1,2}, vf8 with LMUL={1,2,4} where source EMUL < 1 and vs2 overlaps the destination register group

Goal: Confirm all widening instructions trap when SEW equals ELEN so destination EEW = 2*SEW exceeds ELEN
Feature Description: Run a widening instruction with SEW = {all supported} and LMUL = {1, 2, 4}; the instruction should trap only when SEW = MAX_SEW (destination EEW = 2*SEW > ELEN), including widening reductions
Bins: all supported SEW x 3 LMUL bins
Spec: The destination EEW must be a supported element width. For widening operations destination EEW = 2*SEW; when SEW = ELEN this exceeds the maximum supported element width and the instruction encoding is reserved.
Normative Rules: ELEN; V_fp_EEW_IEEE_nsupported_rsv; V_supported_eew; epc_op_V_trap; vfwredosum_op; vfwredusum_op; vstart_op_V_trap; vwop_vd_eew_emul; vwredsum_op; vwredsumu_op

Goal: Confirm vsetvl{i} with rs1=x0 rd=x0 traps when VLMAX would change or vill was set
Feature Description: Run vsetvli with rs1=x0 and rd=x0 when (1) vill=1 beforehand, or (2) new SEW/LMUL would change VLMAX
Bins: 3 crosses: vill=1 case, new SEW differs case, new LMUL differs case
Notes: When rs1=x0 and rd=x0, the instruction operates as if the current vector length in vl is used as the AVL, and the resulting value is written to vl, but not to a destination register. This form can only be used when VLMAX and hence vl is not actually changed by the new SEW/LMUL ratio. Use of the instruction with a new SEW/LMUL ratio that would result in a change of VLMAX is reserved. Use of the instruction is also reserved if vill was 1 beforehand. Implementations may set vill in either case.

Goal: Confirm vsetvl with rs1=x0 rd=x0 traps when VLMAX would change or vill was set
Feature Description: Run vsetvl with rs1=x0 and rd=x0 when (1) vill=1 beforehand, or (2) new SEW/LMUL in rs2 would change VLMAX
Bins: 3 crosses: vill=1 case, new SEW differs case, new LMUL differs case
Notes: When rs1=x0 and rd=x0, the instruction operates as if the current vector length in vl is used as the AVL, and the resulting value is written to vl, but not to a destination register. This form can only be used when VLMAX and hence vl is not actually changed by the new SEW/LMUL ratio. Use of the instruction with a new SEW/LMUL ratio that would result in a change of VLMAX is reserved. Use of the instruction is also reserved if vill was 1 beforehand.

Goal: Confirm segment load/store traps when register groups increment past v31 with LMUL > 1
Feature Description: Run a segment load/store with LMUL={2,4} and NFIELDS/vd combinations where vd + NFIELDS * LMUL > 32 (e.g., LMUL=2 NFIELDS=2 vd=30, LMUL=4 NFIELDS=2 vd=28)
Spec: If the vector register numbers accessed by the segment load or store would increment past 31, then the instruction encoding is reserved.

Goal: Confirm vsetvli/vsetivli with reserved vlmul=100 encoding sets vill
Feature Description: Run vsetvli or vsetivli requesting vlmul=3'b100 (reserved encoding) and verify vill is set to 1 afterward
Expectation: vlmul = {100}: The use of vtype encodings with LMUL < SEWMIN/ELEN is reserved, but implementations can set vill if they do not support these configurations.

Goal: Confirm indexed load/store traps when index EMUL > 8
Feature Description: Run an indexed load/store with SEW/LMUL/index-EEW combinations where (index_EEW / SEW) * LMUL > 8
Spec: If the EMUL would be out of range (EMUL>8 or EMUL<1/8), the instruction encoding is reserved.
Normative Rules: EEW_SEW_LMUL_dep; EMUL_SEW_LMUL_dep; EMUL_rsv; vector_ls_EMUL; vector_ls_EMUL_rsv; vector_ls_indexed_EMUL; vector_ls_indexed_op

Goal: Confirm indexed load/store traps when index EMUL < 1/8
Feature Description: Run an indexed load/store with SEW/LMUL/index-EEW combinations where (index_EEW / SEW) * LMUL < 1/8
Bins: 12 (vlmul, vsew, width) reserved tuples (ifdef-guarded for fractional LMUL and SEW support)
Spec: If the EMUL would be out of range (EMUL>8 or EMUL<1/8), the instruction encoding is reserved.
Normative Rules: EEW_SEW_LMUL_dep; EMUL_SEW_LMUL_dep; EMUL_rsv; vector_ls_EMUL; vector_ls_EMUL_rsv; vector_ls_indexed_EMUL; vector_ls_indexed_op

Feature Description: Confirm all XLEN bits of rs2 are checked when running vsetvl instruction
Expectation:
Set rs1 nonzero, rs2 = (one hot [all XLEN versions]) and run vsetvl rd, rs1, rs2

Running 1 hot confrims that every single bit is checked in rs2 when running vsetvl for reserved configurations
Bins: vtype.vill becomes 1 when there is a 1 in XLEN:8, otherwise it depends on which configuartions are supported

Goal: Confrim that vd vs2 overlap is reserved
Feature Description:
Run instruction with vd = {v8} = vs2

We run with v8 because this ensures its a legal register for all lmuls and does not overlap with v0 incase mask is active
Spec: vmsof.m, vmsif.m, vmsbf.m: The destination register cannot overlap the source register and, if masked, cannot overlap the mask register ('v0').
column12: Any instruction encoding that violates the overlap constraints is reserved.

Goal: Confirm vstart >= VLMAX traps on any vector instruction with valid vtype
Feature Description: Set vtype to a valid configuration (vill=0) and set vstart >= VLMAX, then execute a vector instruction
